channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: A New Era for Book Publishers: Simon & Schuster's Digital Revolution
item.description: Simon & Schuster's new publisher, Sean Manning, shares his plans to modernize the company into a media powerhouse through digital series and social media presence.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: 11 go-to podcasts that inspire IT industry leaders today
item.description: In today’s rapidly evolving technology landscape, IT leaders must stay informed about industry trends and developments while also understanding the priorities and challenges their executive peers face.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: La Policía de Valencia suma la IA a sus filas para mejorar la respuesta ante catástrofes como la DANA
item.description: El 29 de octubre del ya caduco 2024 una DANA arrasó Valencia sembrando la desolación a lo largo y ancho del territorio. La destrucción se adueñó de la región dejando cicatrices aún patentes en la sociedad a día de hoy.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Google Cloud partners with mLogica to offer mainframe modernization
item.description: Google Cloud has partnered with database and mainframe modernization software provider mLogica to help enterprises modernize their mainframe applications. The partnership will see Google Cloud combine its Dual Run service with mLogica’s LIBER*M automated code refactoring platform.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Cleveland Clinic and G42 join forces to drive AI-Powered healthcare innovation
item.description: Cleveland Clinic and G42 have entered into a strategic collaboration to advance the adoption of artificial intelligence (AI) in healthcare, marking yet another major step in realizing AI’s potential in medicine.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: A Venus flytrap wasp? Scientists uncover an ancient insect preserved in amber that snatched its prey
item.description: Scientists have discovered an ancient wasp with a body like a Venus flytrap to seize and snatch its prey.
item.dc:creator: Adithi Ramakrishnan, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Judge allows newspaper copyright lawsuit against OpenAI to proceed
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A federal judge has ruled that The New York Times and other newspapers can proceed with a copyright lawsuit against OpenAI and Microsoft seeking to end the practice of using their stories to train artificial intelligence chatbots. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DOGE staffer allegedly ran company providing services to hacking group
item.description: US Department of Government Efficiency tech advisor Edward Coristine previously ran a small infrastructure provider that offered services to a cybercriminal group, it has been alleged.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Secure encryption and online anonymity are now at risk in Switzerland – here's what you need to know
item.description: An amendment to the Swiss surveillance law would require VPNs and messaging apps to undermine their privacy and security framework. NymVPN, Proton, and Threema are fighting back.
item.dc:creator: chiara.castro@futurenet.com (Chiara Castro), TechRadar

item.title: Signalgate: There’s an IT lesson here
item.description: You know how IT admins are always warning employees about best practices for security? They’re always mandating which apps to use, which to avoid and which devices can safely connect to corporate networks.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Musk’s X poised for ad sales gain on effort to curry Trump favor
item.description: The social network, formerly Twitter, is projected to generate $1.31 billion in US advertising sales in 2025.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Amazon allure grows with cheaper shares than Apple, Walmart
item.description: Amazon.com Inc. shares are starting to look like a bargain, a word that has rarely been used to describe the stock.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: The Apple rumor machine cranks into gear for iOS 19
item.description: WWDC event marketing intensifies, and as we head toward that event in a little over two months, it looks as if we’re being told to expect a new paint job (aka user interface change)s for iOS.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Nissan is back to its bold best with new EV lineup that's led by a third-generation Leaf – and yes, it's an SUV
item.description: Nissan has revealed that it's planning a third-generation Nissan Leaf, along with an electric update of the popular Micra and Juke models for Europe.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Cyprus to fund desalination plants at hotels to reduce impact of water shortages on tourism
item.description: Cyprus says it will make money available to subsidize construction of private desalination plants at hotels on the tourism-reliant island nation as dwindling fresh water reserves in 108 dams are reaching alarmingly low levels.
item.dc:creator: Menelaos Hadjicostis, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Conservatives embrace Tesla as liberals ditch Elon Musk
item.description: Conservatives are rallying around Tesla while liberals ditch or distance themselves from Elon Musk’s electric vehicles (EVs) in a surprising about-face for a company whose fate is deeply tied to the politics of sustainability and climate issues.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Researchers in limbo as Columbia bows to Trump’s demands in bid to restore $400M federal funding
item.description: Researchers at Columbia University remain in limbo after the Ivy League institution bowed to the Trump administration’s demands for unprecedented changes in a bid to restore some $400 million in federal funds cut.
item.dc:creator: Philip Marcelo, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: OpenAI brings AI image generation directly to ChatGPT
item.description: OpenAI is introducing image generation directly within ChatGPT. Powered by its flagship multimodal model, GPT-4o, the chatbot can now create visuals straight from the chat interface. The feature will initially be available to ChatGPT Plus, Pro, Team, and free users.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Warning for developers, web admins: update Next.js to prevent exploit
item.description: Developers and web admins using the Next.js framework for building or managing interactive web applications should install a security update to plug a critical vulnerability.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Waymo Expands Robotaxi Service to Washington D.C., Eyes Driverless Rides Next Year
item.description: Waymo adds Washington D.C. to its list of U.S. markets, with plans for driverless rides next year, expanding its robotaxi service across the country.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: 20 advanced Android 15 tips
item.description: Like many recent Android versions, Android 15 may seem subtle on the surface. And, to be fair, it certainly isn’t the sort of dramatic interface reinvention we’ve seen from Google in the past. But that doesn’t mean it’s short on efficiency-enhancing treats just waiting to be found.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: A European startup scrubs its attempt to launch an orbital rocket on its first test flight
item.description: OSLO, Norway (AP) — A private European aerospace company scrubbed its attempt on Monday to launch the first test flight of its orbital launch vehicle from Norway.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Managing and monitoring user accounts on Linux
item.description: There are a number of commands on Linux that you can use to manage user accounts and monitor user activity. This post provides details on the commands that you need to know if you are managing a Linux server and the user accounts on that system.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: World Backup Day is March 31
item.description: Are you ready? World Backup Day is March 31, a day that has been recognized every year since it was first set up in 2011.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: OpenTofu becomes the real deal
item.description: In open source, forks often struggle to break free from the shadow of their progenitors. But OpenTofu, the community-driven Terraform fork born from HashiCorp’s licensing upheaval, is writing a different story.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Nvidia: Latest news and insights
item.description: Coverage of Nvidia GTC 2025 With its legacy of innovation in GPU technology, Nvidia has become a dominant force in the AI market. Nvidia’s partners read like a technology who’s who list – e.g.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: GSA’s overhaul of FedRAMP contingent on automation
item.description: The General Services Administration will release details of FedRAMP 2025 today outlining a much different program focused mostly on standards and policies. The post GSA’s overhaul of FedRAMP contingent on automation first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Chinese robot's kung fu moves will make your jaw drop
item.description: A humanoid robot developed in China has transformed from a nimble dancer to performing kung fu moves with surprising precision and balance.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: PEAK:AIO adds power, density to AI storage server
item.description: PEAK:AIO, maker of a dedicated AI storage infrastructure server software, has released a new version of its AI Data Server, with emphasis on performance, density, and sustainability.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Adding, managing and deleting groups on Linux
item.description: User groups on Linux systems are often set up to provide access permissions to specific groups of users who share some responsibility. For example, a particular group of users may be allowed to run commands with superuser privilege or access a group of shared files.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Top House Dem seeks details on FOIA staffing amid agency firings
item.description: Rep. Gerry Connolly (D-Va.) is seeking data on FOIA staffing and compliance across federal agencies, which have long struggled with FOIA staff shortages. The post Top House Dem seeks details on FOIA staffing amid agency firings first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: NHL launches a front office iPad app to modernize roster, contract and salary cap info for teams
item.description: MANALAPAN, Fla. (AP) — The NHL has launched a front office app that modernizes the league’s roster, contract and salary cap information.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN WHYNO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Air Force CIO to retire after 31 years of federal service
item.description: Venice Goodwine, the Department of the Air Force’s CIO, plans to leave in mid-May and take some time off. The post Air Force CIO to retire after 31 years of federal service first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network
